steps.title: Convert g-values into f-values
steps.content:
Do not calculate $g(x)$ directly, because the question asks for the $y$-intercept of $f$. Since $g(x)=\frac{f(x)}{x+3}$, multiply each $g(x)$ value by $x+3$:
  • $f(-33)=4(-30)=-120$
  • $f(-9)=0(-6)=0$
  • $f(27)=6(30)=180$

steps.title: Find f from the points
steps.content:
Use the two converted points $(-33,-120)$ and $(-9,0)$ to find the line for $f$:
$$m=\frac{0-(-120)}{-9-(-33)}=\frac{120}{24}=5$$
Substitute $(-9,0)$ into $f(x)=5x+b$: $$0=5(-9)+b,$$ so $b=45$. Therefore $$f(x)=5x+45.$$

steps.title: Answer the intercept question
steps.content: The $y$-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is $(0,b)$, so it is $(0,45)$. That matches Choice A — $\left(0,45\right)$.
